reasoning: The document demonstrates excellent content fidelity (1.0) and strong programmatic metrics (0.97 shingling recall), confirming full text preservation. While the structural score is lower due to minor heading hierarchy inconsistencies and title duplication in specific sections, these are cosmetic defects that do not block approval. Per the routing instructions, a fidelity composite >= 0.85 with only minor concerns is a directive for automatic approval.

concerns.analysis: The output duplicates the text of section titles by including them both in the section's 'title' property and as a redundant 'heading' element within the section's content. This occurs for sections 1, 6, and 13. For example, in Section 1, the title property is '1. AGENCY NAME:' and the first element is a heading with text '1. AGENCY NAME: ____________________________________________________________________________'. Similar duplication occurs in sections 6 and 13.

6. SIGNATURE OF LICENSEE OR PERSON WITH SIGNATORY AUTHORITY: The undersigned...

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and broken. While the document starts with an H2 title, only sections 1, 6, and 13 are assigned H3 heading elements. All other top-level sections (2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22) lack heading elements entirely, appearing only as metadata in the section title property. This prevents screen reader users from navigating the full document structure via headings, as most major sections are omitted from the heading map.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Sections 1 through 22 are all formatted as major section headers in the source document.
concerns.output_quote: Section 1 contains 'heading-2' (Level 3). Section 2 contains only a 'paragraph' element. Section 6 contains 'heading-3' (Level 3). Section 7 contains only a 'paragraph' element.
